title.zh: 第九回 不死之淵 玄母示價
title.en: Chapter 9: The Water of Undying; Lady Silence Shows Its Price

en: The water that will not die brings grief to the one who drinks
The mother of silence reveals the true mechanism

passages.zh: 卻說吳烈孤身抵達天下之盡，玄都入夜後如墨染，城闕無燈，然行路自明，光從何來，不可知也。無言君端坐深殿之中，其前已有人等候多時。殿無香，無火，無侍從之聲，唯四壁隱透一種幽藍，如水底望天。吳烈步入時，膝下忽覺沉重，非恐懼所致，乃此地重力本異於人間，每一步皆叫人想起自身之重——所有過往之事，所有尚未忘卻之人，盡壓於肩上，無聲無形，重於甲冑。
passages.en: Wu Lie had come alone to the world's uttermost edge. After nightfall the Dark Metropolis was ink-dyed through: its towers and gates bore no lanterns, yet the road showed itself clearly — from what source the light came, none could say. Lady Silence sat upright in the innermost hall, where someone had long been expected. The hall held no incense, no fire, no sound of attendants; only the four walls breathed a deep blue luminescence, as if one looked up at the sky from the bottom of water. As Wu Lie stepped inside, a heaviness descended on his knees — not from fear, but because the gravity of this place differed fundamentally from the world of the living. Every step made him conscious of his own weight: all that had passed, all those he had not yet managed to forget, pressed upon his shoulders without shape or sound, heavier than armour.

passages.zh: 無言君不語。吳烈亦不語。半晌，吳烈開口，言辭較來路上所預備者少了許多。他只說：「吾有一問。」無言君闔目，以靜作答。吳烈又道：「不死之水，果有其物乎？」無言君啟眸，目光如淵，不含褒貶，只是望他——如鏡面望人，映出者不是容顏，而是問題本身，問問者為何而問，問者之心，究竟藏著何物。吳烈被那目光照入，心中諸般說辭忽然散去，剩下的不過是一個赤裸的事實：璟同已死。昭明在世。雲素華在世。自己還在。這便是他前來的緣由，全部。
passages.en: Lady Silence did not speak. Wu Lie did not speak. After a long while, Wu Lie opened his mouth, and the words were far fewer than those he had prepared along the road. He said only: 'I have one question.' Lady Silence closed her eyes and answered with stillness. Wu Lie continued: 'The Water of Undying — does such a thing truly exist?' Lady Silence opened her eyes. Her gaze was deep as an abyss, without judgement of any kind; she simply looked at him — as a mirror-surface looks at a person, reflecting not the face but the question itself, and the reason the questioner has asked it, and what it is the questioner's heart truly conceals. Under that gaze Wu Lie's various prepared speeches dissolved; what remained was nothing more than a bare fact: Jing Tong was dead. Zhao Ming was alive. Yun Suhua was alive. He himself remained. This was the entire reason he had come. Nothing more.

redComment.zh: 無言君之眼，實是作者之眼也。此處鏡喻，非照容顏，乃照動機——吳烈欲求不死，其底下藏者，乃不敢再失之心。批者按：人之所求，往往非其所言，無言君先知此理，故以沉默問之。
redComment.en: Lady Silence's eyes are in truth the author's own eyes. The mirror figure here reflects not the face but the motive — beneath Wu Lie's desire for undying life lies a terror of loss he dare not name again. The commentator notes: what a person seeks is rarely what a person says; Lady Silence knows this truth first, and therefore questions him with silence.

zh: 英雄千里來一問，問在唇邊意已違。
鏡中無顏映者何，悲字未出淚先歸。
en: A hero travels a thousand li to ask one question; by the time it reaches the lips, the meaning has already shifted.
The mirror holds no face — what then does it reflect? The word 'grief' is not yet spoken, yet the tears have already turned home.

passages.zh: 無言君起身，不發一語，引吳烈穿行深殿之後，越數道無門之門，至一所在。其地無牆無頂，夜天壓低如帷，星辰在頭頂密密聚攏，大如拳石，近如可摘。地心有一潭，廣不過三丈，水色如鏡，深不可測。無風而水不動，水面映出星辰，與頭頂所見分毫不差，唯方向顛倒，彷彿兩個世界正以此水為界相望。吳烈立於潭邊，下望良久，忽覺心中舊事皆在水中重現——桃林之盟、朱華苑中的燈、璟同臨死之臉——皆清晰如刻，不曾因歲月而模糊半分。
passages.en: Lady Silence rose and, without uttering a word, led Wu Lie through the deepest reaches of the hall, past several doorless doorways, to a certain place. There were no walls here, no ceiling; the night sky pressed low like a curtain, and the stars gathered thickly overhead, large as fist-stones, near enough to pluck. At the centre of the ground lay a pool, no wider than three zhang, its water mirror-coloured, its depth unplumbable. Without any wind, the water did not stir; the water's surface mirrored the stars above in precise correspondence, reversed in direction only, as though two worlds faced each other across this water as their boundary. Wu Lie stood at the pool's edge and looked down for a long time. Suddenly all the old events of his heart re-emerged in the water — the oath in the peach grove, the lamp in the Vermilion Garden, Jing Tong's face at the moment of dying — all of them clear as carving, not blurred even slightly by the passing of years.

passages.zh: 無言君立於他身側，以手勢示意：此即不死之淵。語氣或有或無，聽者自行補足。吳烈問：「飲之果可不死？」無言君點首。吳烈再問：「代價幾何？」無言君這才開口，聲音低平如水底石，她說：「此水非贈，乃借。它從汝所愛之人的餘年中默取，不告知彼，不可贖回。汝之壽延一載，彼之命縮一載。汝飲一口，彼之燈減一寸油，彼不知，汝知。」吳烈立時沉默。無言君繼續道：「此外，不死者所得，非逃離悲苦，乃悲苦之永恆清醒——汝將目睹汝所愛之人一一老去，一一離世，而汝之記憶不會磨損，每一張面孔，每一句話，永遠鮮活如初見，永遠疼痛如初失。」
passages.en: Lady Silence stood at his side and indicated with a gesture: this is the Water of Undying. Whether her meaning was spoken aloud or remained unspoken, the listener supplied what was needed. Wu Lie asked: 'If one drinks it, can one truly not die?' Lady Silence inclined her head. Wu Lie asked again: 'What is the price?' Only then did Lady Silence open her mouth; her voice was low and level as a stone beneath water. She said: 'This water is not a gift — it is a loan. It draws in silence upon the remaining years of those whom you love. They are not informed; it cannot be redeemed. For every year your life is extended, their life is shortened by one year. For every mouthful you drink, their lamp loses a measure of oil. They will not know. You will know.' Wu Lie fell immediately silent. Lady Silence continued: 'Furthermore, what the undying one receives is not escape from grief and suffering, but grief's eternal, clear-eyed extension — you will watch those you love grow old one by one, depart one by one, and your memory will not wear down. Every face, every word spoken, will remain as vivid as the first meeting, as painful as the first loss, forever.'

redComment.zh: 四字乃本回之膽也。「彼不知」者，所愛之人蒙而不覺，其命縮而不知；「汝知」者，飲者獨負此罪，無人可訴，無處可解。此非懲罰，乃結構使然——愛即孤獨，不死即永孤。批者擱筆，不能復言。
redComment.en: These four characters are the courage of this entire chapter. 'They will not know' — the beloved are shielded in ignorance while their lives diminish unseen. 'You will know' — the drinker bears this guilt alone, with no one to confess to, no place of release. This is not punishment; it is the structure of the thing itself — to love is to be alone, and to be undying is to be alone forever. The commentator sets down the brush and cannot speak further.

zh: 不死之水照雙星，一借一還無聲行。
所愛燈油減一寸，飲者孤知天下驚。
記憶不磨刀不鈍，每逢忌日痛如生。
長生非壽是長刑，刑場無獄囚自成。
en: The water of undying mirrors twin stars; the loan and the return are transacted without sound.
The beloved's lamp oil is lessened by one measure; the drinker alone knows, and the knowing shakes the world.
Memory does not wear, the blade does not dull; each anniversary of loss is as painful as the first dying.
Long life is not longevity — it is a long sentence; the execution ground has no prison, yet the prisoner makes himself.

passages.zh: 吳烈立於淵邊，不動，不言。無言君亦退三步，立於稍遠之處，不催，不議，只讓夜色靜靜圍籠。吳烈心中想起昭明。想起昭明在謀事時的神色——眉間一道淺紋，眼底一縷憂光，此後又要因他這個主公之故，再添幾許白髮。他若飲此水，昭明之命便如燭焰，為他而縮。他又想起雲素華，想起她伏案之姿，想起她的簿冊，那些密密的字跡記錄著人世的一切，若她不在了，那記錄便斷，世界的某一部分便無人見證。吳烈蹲下身，以指尖觸及水面。水涼，靜，如璟同最後的額頭。他縮回手。
passages.en: Wu Lie stood at the edge of the pool. He did not move; he did not speak. Lady Silence stepped back three paces and stood a little further away — not hurrying him, not commenting, only letting the night quietly enclose them. In his heart Wu Lie thought of Zhao Ming. He thought of Zhao Ming's expression when planning — a faint crease between the brows, a thread of anxious light in the depths of the eyes; from this point on, because of him, this lord of his, how many more white hairs would appear. If Wu Lie drank this water, Zhao Ming's life would be like a candle flame, shortened on his behalf. Then he thought of Yun Suhua: he thought of her bent over her desk, of her ledgers, those densely written characters that recorded everything of the human world. If she were gone, that record would break off; some portion of the world would go unwitnessed. Wu Lie crouched down and touched the surface of the water with his fingertip. The water was cool and still — like Jing Tong's forehead at the last.

passages.zh: 第一日，吳烈坐於淵邊，未飲。無言君不在，然吳烈知她在某處，如山知天氣之在，不見而感得到。夜間星辰低移，水中星辰亦隨之低移，兩個天空緩緩旋轉，而潭水本身始終寂然如故。吳烈入眠，夢中見璟同，璟同站在桃林中，枝上無花，只剩枯木，璟同笑著說：「你來做什麼？」吳烈欲答，夢已散。
passages.en: The first day: Wu Lie sat beside the pool and did not drink. Lady Silence was not present, yet Wu Lie knew she was somewhere nearby — as a mountain knows the weather without seeing it. In the night the stars moved slowly lower; the stars in the water moved slowly lower with them; two skies turned at a measured pace, while the pool itself remained always as still as before. Wu Lie slept. In his dream he saw Jing Tong. Jing Tong stood in the peach grove; the branches bore no flowers, only bare wood. Jing Tong was smiling. He said: 'What have you come here to do?' Wu Lie tried to answer. The dream dissolved.

passages.zh: 第二日，吳烈仍坐。他不飢，不渴，唯覺寒，然此寒並非風之所賜，乃靜之所賜。靜到極處，人方能聽見平日被雜音遮蓋的東西——他聽見自己的心跳，聽見遠方雲素華執筆的聲音（此當然是幻，然他確乎聽見），聽見昭明某夜喃喃自語的謀算聲（亦是幻，然同樣清晰）。他坐在世界的盡頭，而世界仍在他的耳邊繼續，不曾因他的離開而停止一刻。這令他既感到被需要，又感到渺小。
passages.en: The second day: Wu Lie still sat. He was neither hungry nor thirsty, only cold — yet this cold was not the gift of wind but of stillness. When stillness reaches its extreme, a person begins to hear what ordinary noise has been covering: he heard his own heartbeat; he heard, from far away, the sound of Yun Suhua's brush moving on paper — which was of course hallucination, yet he heard it distinctly; he heard Zhao Ming murmuring calculations to himself on some night — also hallucination, yet equally clear. He sat at the world's farthest edge, and the world continued beside his ear, unceasing, not paused for even a moment by his departure. This made him feel needed and, simultaneously, very small.

zh: 第二日靜如無物，風不至，水不鳴。
素華筆聲傳萬里，昭明謀語入空庭。
皆是幻，皆是真，幻與真間何處停。
en: The second day, still as if nothing existed — no wind arrived, the water made no sound.
Suhua's brush-sound carries ten thousand li; Zhao Ming's planning-words enter the empty courtyard.
All hallucination; all true — between hallucination and truth, where does one rest?

passages.zh: 第三日，吳烈未動。黎明前，天與水之間那道界限幾乎消失，星辰上下皆同，分不清哪個是真實的天空，哪個是鏡中的倒影。吳烈就在這分不清的時刻，向無言君問出那個問題。他不回頭，對著潭水說：「世間可有什麼，是真正不必付代價的？」無言君的聲音從他身後傳來，仍是那個低平的聲調，不急不緩：「有。記憶。它耗費的是記憶者自身，而非被記憶者。」吳烈沉默，久之，他點頭，如一棵樹在無風的日子裡點頭，原因不可見，然動作確實發生。
passages.en: The third day: Wu Lie had not moved. Before dawn, the boundary between sky and water nearly vanished; stars above and below were the same, and one could not distinguish which was the true sky and which was the reflection in the mirror. It was in this moment of indistinction that Wu Lie spoke his question to Lady Silence. He did not turn around; he addressed the pool: 'Is there anything in the world that truly costs nothing?' Lady Silence's voice came from behind him, still in that low and level tone, neither hurried nor slow: 'There is. Memory. It costs the one who remembers — not the one who is remembered.' Wu Lie was silent. After a long time, he nodded — as a tree nods on a windless day, the cause invisible, yet the movement unmistakably real.

redComment.anchor: 記憶。它耗費的是記憶者自身，而非被記憶者
redComment.zh: 此乃全書至此最要緊的一句話，亦是吳烈轉身之樞紐。不死之水奪人壽，記憶只耗自身——故記憶非剝削，而是一種孤獨的尊嚴。吳烈聽此一語，知璟同之死不可逆，然璟同之存在，可以靠他自己來承擔，無需向他人借。批者以為，此句亦是作者對讀者的囑咐：請記住你所愛的人，以你自己的代價，而非他們的。
redComment.en: This is the most essential sentence in the book to this point, and the pivot on which Wu Lie turns homeward. The Water of Undying takes another's years; memory costs only oneself — and so memory is not predation but a solitary dignity. Hearing this single sentence, Wu Lie understands that Jing Tong's death cannot be reversed, yet Jing Tong's existence is something he himself can bear — without borrowing from another. The commentator believes this sentence is also the author's instruction to the reader: remember those you love, at your own cost, not theirs.

zh: 三日淵邊未沾唇，星沉星起見無痕。
不死之水非不死，只是悲苦換長春。
玄母一語輕如水，重過千山壓此身。
英雄轉身向來路，身後淵中仍照人。
en: Three days beside the pool and his lips were never wetted; stars sank and stars rose and left no mark.
The water that will not die is not undying — it only exchanges grief for an extended spring.
Lady Silence speaks one phrase, light as water; it weighs heavier than a thousand mountains upon this body.
The hero turns and faces the road home; behind him, in the pool, the water still holds a reflection of a man.

passages.zh: 吳烈起身，拂衣，未回頭再看那潭水。無言君立於他身後三步，他對她行一揖，言辭簡短：「承蒙指引。」無言君低首，算是答禮，仍不多言。吳烈轉身，走出那無頂之地，走回深殿，走過無門之門，步伐較來時穩了些，非因輕鬆，乃因心中有了去向。他所揹負的並未減輕——璟同之死仍在，然那重量如今有了形狀，他知道如何攜帶它，不必飲水，不必借別人的命，只需一步一步，用自己的腳，走回尚有昭明與雲素華的地方去。
passages.en: Wu Lie stood up, smoothed his garments, and did not look back at the pool. Lady Silence stood three paces behind him; he made a bow toward her and spoke briefly: 'I am grateful for the guidance.' Lady Silence inclined her head in acknowledgement, still saying nothing further. Wu Lie turned and walked out of that roofless place, back through the innermost hall, back through the doorless doors. His step was somewhat steadier than when he had arrived — not because he was lighter, but because his heart now had a direction. What he carried had not lessened — Jing Tong's death was still there — yet that weight had acquired a shape. He knew now how to carry it: without drinking the water, without borrowing another's life, only step by step, on his own feet, walking back toward the place where Zhao Ming and Yun Suhua still were.

passages.zh: 此後無言君獨立殿中，四壁幽藍如故，不死之淵亦如故，星辰在水中顛倒如故。她已見過無數來者，有飲而後悔者，有飲而忘悔者，有如吳烈般三日不飲、轉身而去者——然此最後一類，寥寥無幾。她不知吳烈此後命途如何，天命之事，連玄都之主亦不能逆觀。然她知一事：此人來時帶著一個問題，去時帶著一個答案；答案非她所給，乃他自潭水三日之靜中，自己取得。欲知後事如何，且看下回分解。正是：悲者未必求死，求死者未必悲；三日不飲水，此生方知味。
passages.en: Afterward Lady Silence stood alone in the hall. The deep-blue luminescence of the four walls remained as before; the Water of Undying remained as before; the stars upended in the water remained as before. She had seen numberless petitioners come: some who drank and then repented; some who drank and forgot repentance; some like Wu Lie who for three days did not drink and then turned and departed — yet this last kind was very rare. She did not know what road Wu Lie's fate would take from here; in matters of Heaven's Mandate, not even the ruler of the Dark Metropolis could see backward from the end. Yet she knew one thing: this man had arrived carrying a question, and departed carrying an answer. The answer was not hers to give — he had drawn it himself, from three days of stillness beside the pool. As for what happened next, the following chapter will reveal all. As it truly is: the one in grief does not necessarily seek death; the one who seeks death is not necessarily in grief. Three days beside the water, undrinking — only then does this life begin to know its own flavour.
